title: Direct Customer To Preferred Interfaces Instead Of Performing Removal In Geek
summary: When a customer needs a removal-related action, direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface instead of performing the removal directly in Geek. The source states that removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.
when_to_use: Use this guidance when handling a customer discussion or request involving a removal-related action and the action is being considered directly in Geek.
not_for: Do not use this as a procedure for performing the removal directly in Geek.
Do not use this as a detailed operating procedure for AVEVA or the host/hospital interface, because those detailed steps are not provided in the source.
Do not use this to explain additional system behavior beyond the source statement that removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify when a removal-related action is being considered in Geek, direct the customer to the preferred interface named in the source, explain the source-supported reason, and record that guidance was given.

safety_notes: Use only the source-supported guidance: direct the customer to AVEVA or the host/hospital interface instead of performing the removal directly in Geek.
Do not invent additional removal steps, controls, or system effects not stated in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Knowledge of the preferred interfaces named in the source: AVEVA or host/hospital
Awareness of whether the action is being attempted in Geek

system_preconditions: A customer removal-related action is under discussion or consideration.
The action is being considered directly in Geek or there is uncertainty about which interface should be used.

visual_references.description: Training video frame associated with the discussion warning against direct removal actions in this interface and advising use of approved tools such as AVEVA or the portal/hospital workflow.

steps.title: Identify whether the removal-related action is being considered in Geek
steps.purpose: Determine whether the customer is about to use or is asking support to use Geek for a removal-related action covered by the source caution.
steps.instruction: During the customer discussion, confirm whether the requested removal-related action is being considered directly in Geek.
steps.expected_result: It is clear whether the action is being considered in Geek.
steps.healthy_condition: The support person identifies that the request involves a removal-related action and recognizes whether Geek is the interface being considered.
steps.failure_condition: It remains unclear which interface is being considered, or the situation cannot be mapped to the source guidance.

actions.label: Confirm interface under consideration
actions.exact_action: When discussing a removal-related action with the customer, identify whether the action is being considered directly in Geek.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training frame and associated discussion about not removing items directly in this interface and instead directing customers to approved tools.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required customer-facing interface is unavailable or unclear from the current situation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users should direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface; removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

steps.title: Direct the customer to the preferred interface
steps.purpose: Ensure the customer uses the source-preferred interface instead of performing the action directly in Geek.
steps.instruction: Direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface instead of performing the removal directly in Geek.
steps.expected_result: The customer is redirected to one of the preferred interfaces named in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The customer is instructed to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface and the action is not performed directly in Geek.
steps.failure_condition: The customer is not redirected, or the action proceeds directly in Geek despite the source guidance.

actions.label: Provide interface guidance
actions.exact_action: Use the source guidance to direct the customer to AVEVA or the host/hospital interface instead of performing the removal directly in Geek.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The associated training discussion advising support to direct customers to AVEVA or the portal/hospital workflow rather than removing items directly in this interface.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required customer-facing interface is unavailable or unclear from the current situation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users should direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface; removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

steps.title: Explain why Geek should not be used for this removal
steps.purpose: Provide the source-supported rationale for redirecting the customer away from Geek.
steps.instruction: Explain that removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.
steps.expected_result: The customer receives the source-supported reason for using the preferred interface.
steps.healthy_condition: The explanation stays limited to the source statement and supports the redirection to the preferred interface.
steps.failure_condition: Additional unsupported system behavior is stated, or the reason for redirection is not communicated.

actions.label: State source-supported reason
actions.exact_action: Explain that removing a box in Geek does not send all the information, based on the source statement.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and avoid further explanation if doing so would require inventing additional system behavior beyond the source statement.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users should direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface; removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

steps.title: Record that the customer was directed to the preferred interface
steps.purpose: Capture that the customer was guided to the source-preferred interface for the removal-related action.
steps.instruction: Record that the customer was directed to the preferred interface named in the source.
steps.expected_result: A record exists that the customer was directed to AVEVA or the host/hospital interface.
steps.healthy_condition: The interaction record reflects that the customer was directed to the preferred interface named in the source.
steps.failure_condition: No record is made, or the record does not reflect the source-supported guidance.

actions.label: Document customer direction
actions.exact_action: Record that the customer was directed to the preferred interface named in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users should direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface; removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

success_criteria: The customer is directed to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface.
The removal-related action is not performed directly in Geek when this source guidance applies.
The source-supported reason is communicated: removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.
The interaction is recorded as having directed the customer to the preferred interface.
healthy_conditions: Support recognizes when a removal-related action is being considered in Geek.
The customer is redirected to AVEVA or the host/hospital interface.
Explanations remain limited to source-supported statements.
failure_conditions: The action is performed directly in Geek despite the source guidance.
The preferred interface is unavailable or unclear.
Support adds unsupported claims about system behavior.
The customer direction is not documented.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required customer-facing interface is unavailable or unclear from the current situation.
Escalate rather than inventing additional system behavior or unsupported instructions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training frame tied to the discussion warning against direct removal in this interface and recommending approved customer-facing tools.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Users should direct the customer to use AVEVA or the host/hospital interface; removing a box in Geek does not send all the information.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed steps inside AVEVA.
The source does not provide detailed steps inside the host/hospital interface.
The source does not define the exact documentation system or record format for recording that the customer was directed to the preferred interface.
The source does not specify whether AVEVA and host/hospital are interchangeable in all cases or how to choose between them.
The source does not provide a time estimate.
